step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("effectiveness of geriatric oncology consultations in modifying actual treatment plans for older adults with cancer")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

(1) Briefly state agreement/disagreement with prior conclusion (with one new reason).
(2) Clearly indicate your final answer choice using boxed format.
I agree with the initial conclusion that geriatric oncology consultations do modify cancer treatment plans for elderly patients. The retrieved literature confirms that these consultations incorporate comprehensive geriatric assessments (CGAs) to evaluate functional status, comorbidities, and life expectancy — key determinants used to adjust therapy intensity, reduce inappropriate treatments, and prioritize patient-centered goals aligned with realistic prognosis and quality of life.
